title: Boolean Parenthesization Count
hints: Notice how any full parenthesization splits the expression into two smaller sub-expressions at some operator. Can you define a recursive relationship based on this split?
Consider dynamic programming where you store the number of ways a substring evaluates to true and to false separately for each range.
When combining results from left and right sub-ranges, multiply the specific true/false counts based on the operator's truth table to accumulate totals.
